title: Equifax Completes Acquisition of LawLogix
text: Acquisition Expands Equifax Workforce Solutions Business Unit and Bolsters Company's Robust Portfolio of Employer Solutions ATLANTA , Aug. 31, 2022  /PRNewswire/ --  Equifax ® (NYSE: EFX) announced today that it has completed the acquisition of LawLogix, a leading provider of cloud-based I-9 software and immigration case management software. The definitive agreement to acquire LawLogix was announced on July 21, 2022.

title: Equifax Completes Acquisition of Midigator
text: Acquisition Further Expands Equifax Global Footprint in Digital Identity and Fraud Prevention Solutions ATLANTA , Aug. 26, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Equifax ® (NYSE: EFX) announced today that it has completed the acquisition of Midigator LLC , a provider of post-transaction fraud mitigation solutions. The definitive agreement to acquire Midigator was announced on July 26, 2022.

title: Equifax Announces Enhancements to its Manual Verification Offerings for Mortgage Lenders
text: New Reverification Options Harness Automation to Support a Seamless Customer Experience ATLANTA , Aug. 24, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Today, Equifax ® (NYSE: EFX) announced new options for mortgage lenders leveraging its manual verifications of employment and income from Workforce Solutions, including new automated ordering of reverifications of employment (RVOE) and additional Priority Next Day™ and Priority Two Day™ options. By offering an RVOE with a verification of employment (VOE) or verification of income (VOI) solution, Equifax is helping lenders to satisfy government-sponsored enterprise (GSE) or internal reverification requirements while reducing friction in the lending process.

title: Appriss Insights, an Equifax Company, Launches Enhancements to Background Screening Tools CrimSmart™ and TraceSmart™
text: Enhancements Designed to Provide a More Robust and Streamlined Solution for CRAs ATLANTA , Aug. 22, 2022 /PRNewswire/ --  Appriss Insights , an Equifax®  (NYSE: EFX) company, has launched enhancements to its CrimSmart™ and TraceSmart™ products, which are solutions designed to help background screening firms provide faster results and reduce dead-end investigations, shrink coverage gaps, and expand address history data used by background screeners. "CrimSmart and TraceSmart are changing how the background screening industry gathers and optimizes national-criminal and address history data," said Brian Matthews, Senior Vice President and General Manager of Appriss Insights, an Equifax company.

title: Why Is Equifax (EFX) Up 6.1% Since Last Earnings Report?
text: Equifax (EFX) reported earnings 30 days ago. What's next for the stock?

title: SEC Charges 3 in Connection With 2017 Equifax Data Breach
text: Three individuals have been charged with illegally tipping and trading in the securities of Equifax before its public announcement of a big cyber intrusion and data breach from 2017, the SEC reported.

title: U.S. SEC charges 3 people with insider trading related to Equifax hack
text: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission said on Tuesday it had charged three individuals for illegally tipping and trading in the securities of Equifax Inc before the company announced it had experienced a massive data breach.

title: Enerflex Ltd. (ENRFF) CEO Marc Rossiter on Q2 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: Enerflex Ltd. (OTCPK:ENRFF) Q2 2022 Earnings Conference Call August 11, 2022 10:00 AM ET Company Participants Stefan Ali - Vice President, Strategy and Investor Relations Marc Rossiter - President and Chief Executive Officer Sanjay Bishnoi - Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Ben Park - Vice President, Corporate Controller Conference Call Participants Tim Monachello - ATB Capital Markets Aaron MacNeil - TD Securities Andrew Bradford - Raymond James Michael Robertson - National Bank Financial John Gibson - BMO Nesbitt Burns Operator Good morning, ladies and gentlemen and welcome to Enerflex Second Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call.

title: Rep. Waters: Hold Equifax, Leadership Accountable for Erroneous Credit Scores
text: Rep. Maxine Waters (D-Calif.) has said credit reporting firm Equifax and its leaders should be held accountable for selling erroneous credit scores to lenders, and should be prevented from selling reports until it proves problems were fixed and controls are in place.

title: Opinion: It's not just Equifax. The whole credit reporting system needs an overhaul
text: America's credit reporting system is broken. We saw the latest evidence of this last week, when news broke that Equifax sent lenders millions of incorrect credit scores, some of which were off by at least 25 points, for consumers applying for auto loans, mortgages and credit cards.

title: House Committee Chairwoman Calls for CFPB to Take Action Against Equifax
text: Maxine Waters told the CFPB that it shouldn't let Equifax sell credit scores to lenders until it can prove it has the controls to ensure its scores are accurate.

title: Worried About Your Equifax Credit Score? What to Do
text: Some people applying for mortgages, auto loans and credit cards had their scores changed by 20 points or more.

title: How to find out if the Equifax credit score error affected you
text: CNN's Rahel Solomon explains how would-be borrowers can find out if an incorrect Equifax credit score error affected them and what to do to fix it.

title: Equifax issued the wrong credit scores to millions of consumers
text: Credit giant Equifax sent lenders incorrect credit scores for millions of consumers this spring, in a technology snafu with major real-world impact.

title: Equifax Gave Millions of US Loan Seekers Inaccurate Credit Scores, Report Says
text: The discrepancies were enough to keep some consumers from getting approved for loans, reports The Wall Street Journal.

title: Equifax reveals $25 million stock award to CEO hours after report of inaccurate credit scores
text: Equifax Inc. disclosed that it was giving its chief executive $25 million in a “special” stock award Tuesday, just hours after a report detailed inaccurate credit reports the company sent to consumers.

title: Equifax reportedly gave faulty credit scores that led to higher rates and denied loan applications
text: Equifax provided lenders incorrect credit scores for millions of Americans seeking auto loans, mortgages, and credit cards earlier this year.

title: Equifax Sent Lenders Inaccurate Credit Scores on Millions of Consumers
text: During a three-week period this year, the credit reporting company sent faulty scores to lenders big and small, resulting in higher interest rates and denied applications.
